en.name: Neutrophils in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Neutrophils are the most abundant circulating leukocytes (typically 50–70 % of leukocytes), and the front-line responders of the innate immune system against bacterial and fungal infections [1]. The absolute neutrophil count (ANC) is part of the CBC differential and is critical for assessing risk of bacterial infection: severe neutropenia (ANC < 0.5 ×10⁹/L) is the threshold for febrile-neutropenia management in oncology and intensive care. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a haematology analyser as part of the differential white-cell count, on whole blood (EDTA tube). It is collected in routine biochemistry and intensive care. Results are reported as an absolute number concentration, typically in **`10*9/L`** (NPU SI convention, equivalent to `G/L`) or in the US notation **`10*3/uL`** (numerically identical: `1 ×10⁹/L = 1 ×10³/µL`). ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on the LOINC Group `40654088 / LG32886-0 — Neutrophils|Number Concentration (count/vol)|Moment in time|Blood (LOINC Group)` and pulls in all its descendants. This resolves to three standard concepts, which differ only by counting method. ### Generic (method not specified) - *3017732 / 26499-4 — Neutrophils [#/volume] in Blood (LOINC)*: most widely used LOINC code, returned by haematology analysers when the method is not detailed. ### Method-specific - *3013650 / 751-8 — Neutrophils [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count (LOINC)*: automated impedance, optical or flow-cytometric count (most common in routine practice). - *3017501 / 753-4 — Neutrophils [#/volume] in Blood by Manual count (LOINC)*: manual differential on a stained smear under microscope (used as a confirmation when the analyser flags the result, or in low-resource settings). **Note on units.** Recommended unit: **`10*9/L`** (`9444 / 10*9/L — billion per liter (UCUM)`), aligned with NPU [2]. LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS` is `10*3/uL` (`8848 / 10*3/uL — thousand per microliter (UCUM)`), which is the same physical quantity in US notation. Conversion: `1 ×10⁹/L = 1 ×10³/µL` (factor 1). ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded. The set is defined by anchoring on the LOINC Group for neutrophil number concentration in blood and pulling in all its descendants. This naturally excludes: - **Percentage / fraction variants** — neutrophils as a fraction of total leukocytes (`Neutrophils/100 leukocytes`, `Neutrophils/Leukocytes [Pure number fraction]`) — out of scope, separate measurand. - **Maturation-stage variants** — segmented neutrophils (`Neutrophils.segmented`), band-form neutrophils (`Neutrophils.band form`), immature granulocytes, hyper-/hyposegmented neutrophils — these are subpopulation counts, captured separately if needed. - **Other specimens**: cord blood, fetal blood, body fluid, bone marrow, CSF, dialysis fluid, peritoneal/pleural/synovial fluid — out of scope for a "blood" concept set. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "Neutrophils", "Neutro", "PNN" (polynucléaires neutrophiles), "ANC" (absolute neutrophil count) or similar absolute count without specifying the method: `3017732 / 26499-4 — Neutrophils [#/volume] in Blood`. By far the most common. - **Method-specific source**: if the source explicitly states "automated" or "manual", map to the corresponding method-specific concept. Otherwise, default to `3017732 / 26499-4 — Neutrophils [#/volume] in Blood`. - **Source reports segmented + band counts separately**: those belong to subpopulation concept sets, not to the total neutrophil count. If only a total is needed, sum them upstream of mapping or use the total field directly when available. - **Source reports a percentage** (e.g. `65 %` of leukocytes): this is **not** the same measurand. It belongs to a different concept set (neutrophil percentage). - **Unit notation**: source systems may report `10*9/L`, `G/L`, `10*3/uL`, `K/uL`, or `/uL` (×1000). All correspond to the same physical quantity and can be converted to the recommended `10*9/L` (factor 1 with `10*3/uL`, factor 0.001 with `/uL`). - **Do not confuse with**: neutrophil percentage, segmented/band neutrophil subpopulations, immature granulocytes, or neutrophils on non-blood specimens — all belong to other concept sets. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 26499-4 — Neutrophils [#/volume] in Blood (Part description for "Neutrophils"). Available: https://loinc.org/26499-4/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
